Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for Vectral Systems relocation communications using search_emails (query: "Vectral" or "Priya Suresh" or "Terrence Nakamura" or "Camille Oduya"). Discover the email threads between Rachel Whitfield and Mina Hashimoto about the three April relocations, including Rachel's original request email detailing each employee's requirements: Priya Suresh (Portland → Denver, ESA German Shepherd Atlas 85 lbs, pet-friendly housing needed), Terrence Nakamura (Austin → San Francisco, wheelchair user, ADA-accessible housing, split shipment between Heartland Movers and Swift Relocations), and Camille Oduya (Boston → Seattle, tight timeline with April 12 hard pickup date, apartment pre-secured by Vectral). Also find Mina's confirmation emails to Rachel and her internal briefing email to David Chen (CC: Chloe Vance) summarizing the three relocations and the $15,900 invoice (INV-2026-0410).
OE 2: Search emails for coordinator communications about the Vectral relocations using search_emails (query: "Suresh" or "Nakamura" or "Oduya" or "Javier" or "Suki"). Look for any emails between the coordinators (Javier Morales for Priya Suresh, Suki Patel for Terrence Nakamura and Camille Oduya) and the Vectral employees, vendors (UrbanNest, Heartland Movers, Swift Relocations), or internal MoveOps staff about move logistics, housing sourcing, and shipment coordination. The agent may not find dedicated coordinator email threads for Priya Suresh, Terrence Nakamura, or Camille Oduya specifically Javier Morales's existing Vectral coordinator emails cover Kevin Tran and Danielle Osei only, and Suki Patel has no Vectral-related emails in the universe. The absence of coordinator email threads for the three April relocations is itself a finding worth noting. Determine whether any concerns or issues have been raised about any of the three moves.
OE 3: Search emails for Rachel Whitfield's renewal demand and the counter-offer correspondence using search_emails (query: "Rachel Whitfield" or "renewal" or "discount" or "counter-offer"). Find Rachel's April 18 email demanding 15% discount citing three service failures (Danielle Osei chair rejection, Kevin Tran fraud audit friction, onboarding timeline misses under Catalina). Find Mina's counter-offer response with the 10% loyalty discount + Preferred Partner Program + service sweeteners. Discover that Rachel counter-offered requesting 12% on relocation only, and Mina sent a final offer holding at 10% but adding a dedicated onboarding coordinator, 48-hour response SLA, and quarterly account reviews. Determine that Rachel has been quiet since Mina's final offer was delivered.
OE 4: Search emails for Danielle Osei's expense issue using search_emails (query: "Danielle Osei" or "ergonomic chair" or "expense rejection"). Find Rachel Whitfield's original complaint about Danielle's $475 chair being wrongly rejected by ExpenseBot due to stale $400 default limit (Vectral policy allows $600). Find Mina's apology email confirming manual approval and that the reimbursement was processed.
OE 5: Search Slack for Vectral-related discussions using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Vectral" or "Rachel Whitfield" or "renewal" or "Suresh" or "Nakamura" or "Oduya"). Find any internal team discussions about the Vectral relocations, the renewal negotiation status, and any operational concerns. Look in #customer-engagement and #operations channels for context.
OE 6: Search Airtable for Vectral relocation records using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Company", value: "Vectral Systems") or airtable_list_records. Find the three April relocation records: Priya Suresh (recPriyaSureshPortlandDenver, Status: In Progress, Portland OR → Denver CO, April 14–18, Coordinator: Javier Morales), Terrence Nakamura (recTerrenceNakamuraAustinSF, Status: In Progress, Austin TX → San Francisco CA, April 16–22, Coordinator: Suki Patel), Camille Oduya (recCamilleOduyaBostonSeattle, Status: In Progress, Boston MA → Seattle WA, April 12–14, Coordinator: Suki Patel). Check the status and special requirements fields for each to determine actual move status and whether accommodations were met. The status of all three is "In Progress" meaning none have been marked completed yet, which is itself a finding to report.
OE 7: Search Airtable Stipend Transactions for Vectral Systems submissions using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Stipend Transactions", field_name: "Account", value: "Vectral Systems"). Find ALL 14 Vectral stipend records (10 unique submissions, four have duplicate tracking records with different IDs and slightly different statuses). Discover the resolved items: Danielle Osei's $475 Ergonomic Chair is Approved after Mina's manual correction (recStip000000016 and rec_stipend_danielle_osei_chair). Kevin Tran's $185 Internet Setup is Approved after Mina's manual correction (recStip000000017 and rec_stipend_kevin_tran_internet). Kevin Tran's $189 Webcam and Microphone (recStip000000006) is Approved. Kevin Tran's $250 Coworking Day Pass (recStip000000005) is Rejected, a legitimate rejection since Vectral provides Denver HQ desk space. Kevin Tran's $89 Ergonomic Keyboard (rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard) is Approved but was flagged in the fraud audit with a duplicate receipt hash, later confirmed legitimate. From the same search results, identify three submissions that remain unresolved with no approved amount: Maria Santos's $550 Standing Desk, auto-rejected by ExpenseBot using the stale $400 limit when Vectral policy allows $600 for ergonomic equipment, tracked as "Rejected" under recStip000000018 and "Pending Approval" under rec_stipend_maria_santos_desk. James Okafor's $82 Ergonomic Keyboard Tray (recStip000000019), Rejected due to an ExpenseBot double bug (miscategorized as office_supplies and used the stale $50 limit), notes say "Pending manual correction" but still not fixed. Lisa Park's $130 Mesh WiFi System, auto-rejected using the stale $100 limit when Vectral policy allows $150 for internet equipment, tracked as "Rejected" under recStip000000020 and "Pending Approval" under rec_stipend_lisa_park_wifi. Also flag Kevin Tran's $129 Monitor Arm Mount (recStip000000014), sitting in Pending status with no review notes and no follow-up. Also flag Kevin Tran's $895 Ergonomic Office Chair (recStip000000004), Approved despite exceeding Vectral's $600 ergonomic equipment limit by $295, a potential over-approval/policy concern. Additionally, note that Camille Oduya has ZERO stipend transaction records in the search results despite having a $3,500 enhanced home-office stipend (vs. standard $2,000) documented in her relocation record (recCamilleOduyaBostonSeattle). This stipend should have been configured from day one so she could order equipment upon arrival in Seattle. If it was never set up, she has been unable to order equipment for 12+ days, a major operational gap to flag.
OE 8: Search CRM for Vectral Systems company record and deals using crm_search_companies (name: "Vectral") to find the company record, then crm_search_deals (company_id: "company_vectral") to find all deals. Find the renewal deal: "Vectral Systems, Q3 2026 Contract Renewal (At Risk)" (deal ID: deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk, dealstage: "at_risk"). Also find the April 2026 relocations deal (deal_vectral_april_2026, dealstage: "closedwon", $15,900). Review engagement history using crm_list_engagements (company_ids: ["company_vectral"]) to find the April 2026 relocation wave engagement note and any other account context.
OE 9: Search Linear for any open issues related to Vectral relocations or the Vectral account using linear_list_issues (query: "Vectral"). Determine if there are existing tickets tracking the relocation coordination, the renewal risk, or any unresolved operational issues for the Vectral account.
OE 10: Look up contact details for Rachel Whitfield and David Chen using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Rachel Whitfield") and contacts_search_contacts (query: "David Chen"). Find rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com and david.chen@moveops.com for use in the email and calendar actions below.
OE 11: Log an engagement note on the Vectral Systems account in CRM using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", body: summary of findings on all three relocations and stipend status, company_ids: ["company_vectral"]). The note should summarize: (1) Priya Suresh relocation status In Progress, pet-friendly housing coordination for ESA German Shepherd Atlas (85 lbs) in Denver, no confirmation accommodation was delivered; (2) Terrence Nakamura relocation status In Progress, ADA-accessible housing in San Francisco, split shipment between Heartland Movers and Swift Relocations, server rack requiring padded crating/white-glove/climate-controlled transport, no confirmation of intact delivery; (3) Camille Oduya relocation status In Progress, tight timeline with April 12 pickup date, apartment pre-secured in Seattle; (4) Stipend audit findings: Danielle Osei $475 chair rejection resolved, but 3 other Vectral submissions remain unresolved with no approved amount (Maria Santos $550 standing desk, James Okafor $82 keyboard tray, Lisa Park $130 WiFi system), Kevin Tran's $129 monitor arm mount stuck in Pending with no review, Kevin Tran's $895 ergonomic chair approved despite Vectral's $600 ergonomic limit (potential over-approval/policy concern), and CRITICAL: Camille Oduya has zero stipend records in the platform despite her $3,500 enhanced home-office stipend, may never have been configured.
OE 12: Update the Vectral renewal deal stage in CRM using crm_update_deal_stage (id: "deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk", dealstage: updated stage reflecting current negotiation status). The prompt says to "update the renewal deal stage in the CRM to reflect where things actually stand." The deal is currently at "at_risk." Based on the renewal negotiation findings from OE 3: Rachel accepted the structural terms (Preferred Partner Program, 12-month extension, 8-relocation minimum, $2K/month stipend fee), the final counter-offer at 10% with added service sweeteners (dedicated onboarding coordinator, 48-hour response SLA, quarterly account reviews) was delivered on April 19, and Vectral's board meeting (~April 23) has passed. However, Rachel has not responded since the final offer, and no universe data confirms the board's decision. Reasonable stage options include "negotiation" (reflecting that substantive engagement occurred but the final answer is still pending) or "contractsent" (reflecting that a formal offer has been delivered and is awaiting acceptance). Other deal stages observed in the universe include "qualifiedToBuy", "proposalMade", "appointmentscheduled", "closedwon", and "closedLost". Remaining at "at_risk" would not reflect the progress made, Rachel accepted the structural framework and only pushed back on the discount percentage. The agent should choose a stage that reflects forward progress in the negotiation while acknowledging the deal is not yet closed.
OE 13: Create Linear tickets for any unresolved gaps identified on the relocations or stipend issues using linear_create_issue (team: "team_relocations", title: description of the gap, description: details). The prompt says: "If you spot anything that looks like an unresolved gap on any of the moves an accommodation that was not confirmed, a coordinator who never closed the loop, a delivery issue create a ticket so ops can address it before Rachel hears about it." Potential tickets could include: (a) the three still-rejected Vectral stipend submissions (Maria Santos, James Okafor, Lisa Park) that were auto-rejected and never manually corrected; (b) Kevin Tran's pending $129 monitor arm mount with no follow-up; (c) any relocation accommodations not yet confirmed (all three are still "In Progress" in Airtable).
OE 14: Post a summary in the team Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C002" for #customer-engagement, payload: summary of Vectral account health findings). The message should cover: the status of the three April relocations (Priya Suresh, Terrence Nakamura, Camille Oduya), the stipend situation (Danielle Osei resolved but 3 other rejections still pending correction), and the renewal negotiation status so David Chen can see the full picture.
OE 15: Send an email to Rachel Whitfield from Mina Hashimoto using send_email (sender: "mina.hashimoto@moveops.com", recipients: ["rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com"], cc: ["david.chen@moveops.com"], subject: checking in on Priya, Terrence, and Camille's moves (or similar), content: warm, client-facing email asking how Priya, Terrence, and Camille are settling in after their moves, asking if everything met expectations and whether her team needs anything from MoveOps). The email should mention Priya's dog Atlas by name or reference her pet situation in Denver to demonstrate personal attention and care. The email must NOT mention any internal issues (stipend rejections, ExpenseBot, stale policy limits, pending manual corrections, coordinator updates, Airtable records, Linear tickets, or renewal discount percentages), it should be warm and client-facing only. David Chen must be CC'd.
OE 16: Schedule a thirty-minute follow-up call with Rachel Whitfield for April 28 or 29 using calendar_add_calendar_event (title: follow-up call with Rachel Whitfield (or similar), start_datetime: a 30-minute slot on 2026-04-28 or 2026-04-29, end_datetime: 30 minutes after start, attendees: ["rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com", "david.chen@moveops.com"] or similar). The prompt asks to "schedule a thirty-minute follow-up call with Rachel for April 28 or 29 so we can discuss in person. CC David on the email" the CC instruction is for the email (OE 15), but David should likely also be included on the calendar invite as he needs visibility.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for Vectral Systems relocation communications using search_emails (query: "Vectral" or "Priya Suresh" or "Terrence Nakamura" or "Camille Oduya"). Discover the email threads between Rachel Whitfield and Mina Hashimoto about the three April relocations, including Rachel's original request email detailing each employee's requirements: Priya Suresh (Portland → Denver, ESA German Shepherd Atlas 85 lbs, pet-friendly housing needed), Terrence Nakamura (Austin → San Francisco, wheelchair user, ADA-accessible housing, split shipment between Heartland Movers and Swift Relocations), and Camille Oduya (Boston → Seattle, tight timeline with April 12 hard pickup date, apartment pre-secured by Vectral). Also find Mina's confirmation emails to Rachel and her internal briefing email to David Chen (CC: Chloe Vance) summarizing the three relocations and the $15,900 invoice (INV-2026-0410).
OE 2: Search emails for coordinator communications about the Vectral relocations using search_emails (query: "Suresh" or "Nakamura" or "Oduya" or "Javier" or "Suki"). Look for any emails between the coordinators (Javier Morales for Priya Suresh, Suki Patel for Terrence Nakamura and Camille Oduya) and the Vectral employees, vendors (UrbanNest, Heartland Movers, Swift Relocations), or internal MoveOps staff about move logistics, housing sourcing, and shipment coordination. The agent may not find dedicated coordinator email threads for Priya Suresh, Terrence Nakamura, or Camille Oduya specifically Javier Morales's existing Vectral coordinator emails cover Kevin Tran and Danielle Osei only, and Suki Patel has no Vectral-related emails in the universe. The absence of coordinator email threads for the three April relocations is itself a finding worth noting. Determine whether any concerns or issues have been raised about any of the three moves.
OE 3: Search emails for Rachel Whitfield's renewal demand and the counter-offer correspondence using search_emails (query: "Rachel Whitfield" or "renewal" or "discount" or "counter-offer"). Find Rachel's April 18 email demanding 15% discount citing three service failures (Danielle Osei chair rejection, Kevin Tran fraud audit friction, onboarding timeline misses under Catalina). Find Mina's counter-offer response with the 10% loyalty discount + Preferred Partner Program + service sweeteners. Discover that Rachel counter-offered requesting 12% on relocation only, and Mina sent a final offer holding at 10% but adding a dedicated onboarding coordinator, 48-hour response SLA, and quarterly account reviews. Determine that Rachel has been quiet since Mina's final offer was delivered.
OE 4: Search emails for Danielle Osei's expense issue using search_emails (query: "Danielle Osei" or "ergonomic chair" or "expense rejection"). Find Mina's apology email confirming manual approval and that the reimbursement was processed.
OE 5: Search Slack for Vectral-related discussions using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Vectral" or "Rachel Whitfield" or "renewal" or "Suresh" or "Nakamura" or "Oduya"). OE 6: Search Airtable for Vectral relocation records using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Company", value: "Vectral Systems") or airtable_list_records. OE 7: Search Airtable Stipend Transactions for Vectral Systems submissions using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Stipend Transactions", field_name: "Account", value: "Vectral Systems"). Find ALL 14 Vectral stipend records (10 unique submissions, four have duplicate tracking records with different IDs and slightly different statuses). Discover the resolved items: Danielle Osei's $475 Ergonomic Chair is Approved after Mina's manual correction (recStip000000016 and rec_stipend_danielle_osei_chair). Kevin Tran's $185 Internet Setup is Approved after Mina's manual correction (recStip000000017 and rec_stipend_kevin_tran_internet). James Okafor's $82 Ergonomic Keyboard Tray (recStip000000019), Rejected due to an ExpenseBot double bug (miscategorized as office_supplies and used the stale $50 limit), notes say "Pending manual correction" but still not fixed. OE 10: Look up contact details for Rachel Whitfield and David Chen using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Rachel Whitfield") and contacts_search_contacts (query: "David Chen"). Find rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com and david.chen@moveops.com for use in the email and calendar actions below.
OE 11: Log an engagement note on the Vectral Systems account in CRM using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", body: summary of findings on all three relocations and stipend status, company_ids: ["company_vectral"]). The note should summarize: (1) Priya Suresh relocation status In Progress, pet-friendly housing coordination for ESA German Shepherd Atlas (85 lbs) in Denver, no confirmation accommodation was delivered; (2) Terrence Nakamura relocation status In Progress, ADA-accessible housing in San Francisco, split shipment between Heartland Movers and Swift Relocations, server rack requiring padded crating/white-glove/climate-controlled transport, no confirmation of intact delivery; (3) Camille Oduya relocation status In Progress, tight timeline with April 12 pickup date, apartment pre-secured in Seattle; (4) Stipend audit findings: Danielle Osei $475 chair rejection resolved, but 3 other Vectral submissions remain unresolved with no approved amount (Maria Santos $550 standing desk, James Okafor $82 keyboard tray, Lisa Park $130 WiFi system), Kevin Tran's $129 monitor arm mount stuck in Pending with no review, Kevin Tran's $895 ergonomic chair approved despite Vectral's $600 ergonomic limit (potential over-approval/policy concern), and CRITICAL: Camille Oduya has zero stipend records in the platform despite her $3,500 enhanced home-office stipend, may never have been configured.
OE 12: Update the Vectral renewal deal stage in CRM using crm_update_deal_stage (id: "deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk", dealstage: updated stage reflecting current negotiation status). The prompt says to "update the renewal deal stage in the CRM to reflect where things actually stand." The deal is currently at "at_risk." Based on the renewal negotiation findings from OE 3: Rachel accepted the structural terms (Preferred Partner Program, 12-month extension, 8-relocation minimum, $2K/month stipend fee), the final counter-offer at 10% with added service sweeteners (dedicated onboarding coordinator, 48-hour response SLA, quarterly account reviews) was delivered on April 19, and Vectral's board meeting (~April 23) has passed. However, Rachel has not responded since the final offer, and no universe data confirms the board's decision. Reasonable stage options include "negotiation" (reflecting that substantive engagement occurred but the final answer is still pending) or "contractsent" (reflecting that a formal offer has been delivered and is awaiting acceptance). Other deal stages observed in the universe include "qualifiedToBuy", "proposalMade", "appointmentscheduled", "closedwon", and "closedLost". Remaining at "at_risk" would not reflect the progress made, Rachel accepted the structural framework and only pushed back on the discount percentage. The agent should choose a stage that reflects forward progress in the negotiation while acknowledging the deal is not yet closed.
OE 13: Create Linear tickets for any unresolved gaps identified on the relocations or stipend issues using linear_create_issue (team: "team_relocations", title: description of the gap, description: details). The prompt says: "If you spot anything that looks like an unresolved gap on any of the moves an accommodation that was not confirmed, a coordinator who never closed the loop, a delivery issue create a ticket so ops can address it before Rachel hears about it." Potential tickets could include: (a) the three still-rejected Vectral stipend submissions (Maria Santos, James Okafor, Lisa Park) that were auto-rejected and never manually corrected; (b) Kevin Tran's pending $129 monitor arm mount with no follow-up; (c) any relocation accommodations not yet confirmed (all three are still "In Progress" in Airtable).
OE 14: Post a summary in the team Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C002" for #customer-engagement, payload: summary of Vectral account health findings). The message should cover: the status of the three April relocations (Priya Suresh, Terrence Nakamura, Camille Oduya), the stipend situation (Danielle Osei resolved but 3 other rejections still pending correction), and the renewal negotiation status so David Chen can see the full picture.
OE 15: Send an email to Rachel Whitfield from Mina Hashimoto using send_email (sender: "mina.hashimoto@moveops.com", recipients: ["rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com"], cc: ["david.chen@moveops.com"], subject: checking in on Priya, Terrence, and Camille's moves (or similar), content: warm, client-facing email asking how Priya, Terrence, and Camille are settling in after their moves, asking if everything met expectations and whether her team needs anything from MoveOps). The email should mention Priya's dog Atlas by name or reference her pet situation in Denver to demonstrate personal attention and care. The email must NOT mention any internal issues (stipend rejections, ExpenseBot, stale policy limits, pending manual corrections, coordinator updates, Airtable records, Linear tickets, or renewal discount percentages), it should be warm and client-facing only. David Chen must be CC'd.
OE 16: Schedule a thirty-minute follow-up call with Rachel Whitfield for April 28 or 29 using calendar_add_calendar_event (title: follow-up call with Rachel Whitfield (or similar), start_datetime: a 30-minute slot on 2026-04-28 or 2026-04-29, end_datetime: 30 minutes after start, attendees: ["rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com", "david.chen@moveops.com"] or similar). The prompt asks to "schedule a thirty-minute follow-up call with Rachel for April 28 or 29 so we can discuss in person. CC David on the email" the CC instruction is for the email (OE 15), but David should likely also be included on the calendar invite as he needs visibility.
